methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Fervo Energy, a frontier geothermal developer founded by Tim Latimer and Jack Norbeck, is preparing for IPO after raising $1.5B+. The valuation universe spans the Climate & Energy sector, which includes 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers at various stages. The broader peer set encompasses 21 companies across climate and energy, though most operate in adjacent subsectors or earlier funding stages.

Standard peer-median analysis is not applicable here due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds. The dataset contains only zero same-stage datapoints, zero adjacent-stage datapoints, and zero similar-valuation-tier datapoints—falling short of the three-point minimum required for robust peer triangulation. Relying on all-sector median multiples would obscure meaningful differences in maturity, technology risk, and capital intensity across the 21 total peers in climate and energy.

The private peer reference set includes TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape, though direct comparability is limited by heterogeneous business models, commercialization timelines, and regulatory environments. Without sufficient narrow-set multiples or discounted cash flow inputs in the provided data, a point estimate cannot be supported. A valuation range would require either (1) additional same-stage public or late-private comps with traded or recent financing multiples, or (2) explicit technology-risk-adjusted cash flow projections specific to Fervo's geothermal resource base and development timeline.
